published.title: How to Set Up a Fully Local Voice Assistant in Home Assistant (No Cloud, No Alexa)
targetKeywords: home assistant local voice assistant no cloud
home assistant voice PE privacy local
published.metaDescription: Set up a fully local, cloud-free voice assistant in Home Assistant using Voice PE, Wyoming protocol, Piper TTS, and openWakeWord—step-by-step.
published.angle: The Home Assistant Voice Preview Edition hardware just shipped and r/homeassistant is buzzing—show readers exactly how to wire up the full local stack (Voice PE → Wyoming → Whisper STT → Piper TTS → openWakeWord) so every spoken command stays on their LAN, with zero Alexa, zero Google, zero Nabu Casa cloud required.

lastSeoCheck.recommendation: Post published today (2026-03-12); not indexed yet — this is entirely normal, expect 24–72h for Google crawl. The primary competitive landscape for 'home assistant local voice assistant no cloud' is dominated by the official home-assistant.io docs (which will always outrank third-party guides on brand terms), XDA Developers, and GriswoldLabs. The Voice PE angle is the right differentiator — official docs cover the generic pipeline; tarahome.ai's post should emphasize the Voice PE hardware-specific wiring (Voice PE → Wyoming → Whisper → Piper → openWakeWord) as no other third-party guide covers that end-to-end stack explicitly. Recommended actions: (1) Submit URL to Google Search Console immediately for faster indexing. (4) Consider adding a 'Voice PE vs generic Wyoming satellite' comparison section to boost uniqueness signals.
